Okay, I'm trying to figure out whether the mutant code change caused any issues with nearby lines. The mutant is at line 91 in DateTimeZone.java. Let me look at what was changed.

Originally, line 91 had a condition checking if minutesOffset is less than 0 or greater than 59. But the mutant changes it to check if minutesOffset is less than 0 or greater than 0. That means any positive value over 0 will throw an error because now minutes can't be more than 0.

Wait, but that doesn't make sense because minutes should be between 0 and 59. So this change would incorrectly reject valid minute values like 30 or 59. This could cause issues in other parts of the code where minutes are supposed to be within a certain range.

Looking at the failing tests, TestDateTimeZone#testForOffsetHoursMinutes_int_int started failing after this mutation. That makes sense because when you pass a valid minute value (like 30), it's now throwing an exception instead of processing it correctly. Also, some other tests like testWithMinuteOfHourInDstChange_mockZone are now failing with the IllegalArgumentException because of this incorrect validation.

So the mutant caused a problem in the minutes offset check, leading to unexpected exceptions and failed tests. This suggests that the mutant introduced a bug by limiting the valid range for minutes incorrectly.
</think>
